title: PG-001: Postgres schema migration — app-layer import switching and dual-write
consumer_purpose: Enable production code to work with both SQLite and Postgres backends, with a dual-write adapter for migration safety
claim_atoms: 1. Create schema-pg.ts barrel exporting from .pg.sql.ts files; 2. Replace all production imports from .sql.ts to .pg.sql.ts via barrel; 3. Add makeDualWriteAdapter() that writes to both backends; 4. All typechecks pass; 5. All existing tests pass
content:
## PG-001: Import Switch + Dual-Write Plan

### Background
- 7 SQLite tables have complete PG equivalents (.pg.sql.ts files)
- Some .pg.sql.ts re-export from .sql.ts (shared schemas — identical column defs)
- Some .pg.sql.ts have full pgTable implementations (dialect-specific types)
- DB adapter layer already supports both SQLite and Postgres backends
- All production code currently imports from .sql.ts (SQLite tables)

### Step 1: Create storage/schema-pg.ts (PG schema barrel)
New barrel that exports all tables from .pg.sql.ts:
- account → account.pg.sql.ts
- project → project.pg.sql.ts
- session → session.pg.sql.ts
- share → share.pg.sql.ts
- workspace → control-plane/workspace.pg.sql.ts
- event → event/event.pg.sql.ts
- data-migration → data-migration.pg.sql.ts
- sync/event → sync/event.pg.sql.ts

### Step 2: Create storage/schema-registry.ts (runtime-switchable barrel)
Factory that returns either SQLite or PG schema set based on config:
- getSchema(kind: "sqlite" | "postgres") → schema set
- Default: SQLite (backward compatible)
- When PG URL is configured: PG schema

### Step 3: Add makeDualWriteAdapter() to adapter.ts
New adapter that wraps both SQLite and PG:
- query: runs on PG, replicates to SQLite (dual-write)
- transaction: PG primary, SQLite shadow
- Graceful PG failure: SQLite fallback
- Config: database.dualWrite boolean

### Step 4: Switch production imports 
Update storage-related imports to go through schema-registry.

### Step 5: Verify
- bun typecheck in packages/opencode
- bun test (if available)
